title: iam-jit ambient declarative config (v1.0)
description: Operator-authored declaration consumed by `iam_jit_setup_from_config` (MCP) and `iam-jit doctor --apply-config` (CLI). One declaration, one block in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules / .iam-jit.yaml — agent reads it on session start and installs + starts + configures the bouncers the operator asked for. v1.1 Phase A: setup-only; v1.1 Phase B will wire `improve.*` semantics (currently accepted but no-op with a warning).

schema_version.description: Optional schema version pin. Defaults to 1.0 when omitted; bumps follow [[config-export-wire-divergence]] (STRING per ibounce/audit-export conventions).

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, `iam_jit_setup_from_config` is a no-op (returns a structured `{status: 'disabled'}` payload) so an operator can flip a single bit to opt every harness out without removing the rest of the declaration.

posture.description: ambient = agent reads this declaration on session start and maintains the bouncer surface; managed = operator manages bouncers manually (declaration is reference-only). v1.0 behavior is `managed`; v1.1 introduces `ambient` per [[ambient-autonomous-protection]].

bouncers.description: Per-bouncer declaration. Every Bounce gets the same shape per [[cross-product-agent-parity]].

improve.description: Phase B (#401) continuous-improvement knobs. Accepted in Phase A for forward-compatibility but `enabled: true` triggers a warning that the feature is not yet wired.

require_operator_approval_above_change_t.description: Profile-diff fraction above which an auto-improve proposal is held for operator approval instead of auto-installed. 0.0 = always hold; 1.0 = never hold.

notify_on_deny.description: Surface block notifications to the operator (stderr / Slack hook depending on `iam-jit --notify-denies` config). Per founder direction 2026-05-23 the deny-obviousness surface MUST stay on by default; setting this false is the explicit operator choice to mute.

fail_on_deny.description: When true, any unexpected deny is treated as a build/operation failure (non-zero exit, raised in CI). Default false for `posture: ambient` (local dev tolerates blocks); per [[ambient-autonomous-protection]] managed posture (CI/CD) typically sets this true so silent drift can't pass through a pipeline. Setting `fail_on_deny: true` with `posture: ambient` emits a warning suggesting `posture: managed`.

require_signed_profiles.description: Optional: only load profiles carrying a valid Ed25519 signature from a trusted publisher. Forward-compat field for the threat-feed + signed-profile work; in v1.0 Phase A this is a declaration field only and is surfaced in `--inspect` output but does not gate profile loading yet (full enforcement ships with #407+#410). Recommended for `posture: managed`.

threat_feed.description: Phase C (#407-#411 §A51-§A55) — operator-pinned threat-feed subscriptions. Per [[independence-as-security-property]] feeds are operator-pinned; the bouncer fetches + verifies + applies per severity. Per [[ambient-autonomous-protection]] `posture: managed` REFUSES auto-apply (CI must explicitly PR-review).

retention.description: #428 / §A67 — compliance retention tiering. Declarative multi-tier retention (hot/warm/cold + optional purge) selected by framework name (pci | hipaa | sox | gdpr | custom) with per-field overrides. Per-framework defaults match industry: PCI 1y, HIPAA 6y, SOX 7y, GDPR variable + PII purge. Wired at bouncer start: write-time PII redaction (gdpr_pii_purge path) + offline tier mover (`iam-jit audit retention apply`). See docs/PRODUCTION-LOG-STORAGE.md §retention.

audit_chain.description: #500 / §A66c — Phase F audit-chain opt-in. When `enabled: true` the AuditLogWriter stamps every audit event with `unmapped.iam_jit.audit_chain.{seq,prev_hash,hash}` so `iam-jit audit verify` can detect edits / re-ordering / deletion. State persists across restarts at <log_dir>/audit-chain-state.json. Symmetric with the --audit-chain CLI flag (either path opts in). Default OFF per [[creates-never-mutates]].

audit_sign_manifests.description: #500 / §A66c — Phase F signed-manifest opt-in. When `enabled: true` the bouncer emits Ed25519-signed manifests every `interval_events` chain rows so TAIL-truncation (which the chain alone cannot catch) is detectable. Requires `audit_chain.enabled: true`. Symmetric with the --audit-sign-manifests CLI flag. Default OFF.

resource_mappings.description: Phase E (#420 §A59) — named resource mappings consumed by `iam-jit resource-map` + `iam_jit_resource_map` (MCP). Operator declares NAMED account/region/name substitution rules; agent picks a name by intent (e.g. 'staging_to_prod') and iam-jit applies pure textual substitution. No inference happens at the iam-jit layer per [[scorer-is-ground-truth]] — the agent is the synthesizer; iam-jit only substitutes per the operator's declaration.

deployment_targets.description: Phase G (#437 §A71) — named deployment-target classifiers consumed by `iam-jit deployment-targets list` + `bounce_deployment_targets_for_filter` (MCP). Operator declares what their deployment-targets ARE (prod-k8s, staging-k8s, prod-aws, ...); agent reads the classifier to scope a long-range audit query (#436) when synthesising a per-target bouncer config. Per [[bouncer-informs-agent-informs-iam-jit]] iam-jit just provides the taxonomy + the long-range audit access; the AGENT does the synthesis. No inference happens at the iam-jit layer — operator authors the scope rules; iam-jit looks them up.

anomaly_detection.description: Phase H (#468-#471 §A76-§A79) — per-agent behavioral baseline + anomaly detector. Per [[anomaly-detection-mode-phase-h]] this is an ADDITIVE operator wedge: opt in to track each agent's baseline + flag actions that are either known-malicious (composes with #404 deny-classifier + #407 threat-feed) or N-sigma away from the agent's historical pattern. Two response modes: `block` (returns structured deny + emits OCSF anomaly_detected) or `alert` (lets through + emits). Per [[independence-as-security-property]] the baseline stays LOCAL; never sent anywhere. Per [[scorer-is-ground-truth]] the detector is ADVISORY — deterministic deny floor wins on conflict.

enabled.description: true / false explicit; OR a `when_X_present` heuristic the setup tool resolves at apply time. Per [[ibounce-honest-positioning]] heuristics MUST be transparent in dry-run output — operator sees exactly what each conditional resolved to.

mode.description: discovery (default per [[discovery-first-default]]) = observe + audit + pass-through; cooperative = audit + advise but always forward; strict = transparent enforcement (DENY → 403).

profile.description: `auto` = use the currently-installed/active profile; named profile = pin to that named profile (must exist in profiles.yaml). When `auto` + nothing installed, falls back to the bouncer's discovery default per [[discovery-first-default]].

port.description: Override the bouncer's default port. Optional — when omitted the bouncer-specific default is used (ibounce 8767, kbouncer 8766, dbounce mgmt 8768 / wire 5433, gbounce mgmt 8769 / wire 8080).

profile_source.description: Path or URL to the canonical profile file. REQUIRED per enabled bouncer when the parent declaration uses `posture: managed`. Local path (`./profiles/ci-staging.yaml`) or HTTPS URL (`https://profiles.example.com/ci-staging.yaml`). Ambient posture ignores this field (the profile is derived/observed); managed posture pins the source so the running bouncer matches the committed file byte-for-byte.

profile_sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the profile file referenced by `profile_source`. Optional but recommended for `posture: managed` — when present the loader verifies the file's hash before applying and refuses to start the bouncer on a mismatch (drift detection). 64 hex chars; case-insensitive.

disk_pressure_mode.description: #424 / §A63 — disk-pressure circuit-breaker reaction mode when audit-log directory crosses the critical threshold. pause-requests (default, compliance-heavy) refuses new agent requests with HTTP 503. rotate-aggressively drops oldest rotated archives to recover space. archive-and-purge emits operator hint that the #317 object-storage sink should ship before dropping. See docs/PRODUCTION-LOG-STORAGE.md §5.1. Per [[cross-product-agent-parity]] kbouncer/dbouncer/gbouncer accept the same string values.

extra_args.description: Extra CLI args passed to the bouncer's `run` command. Use sparingly — the declarative shape should cover the common cases.

threat_feed_block.description: Phase C (#407 §A51) — one pinned threat-feed subscription. Per [[independence-as-security-property]] every subscription is OPERATOR-pinned (URL + publisher pubkey OR cosign identity). Per [[ambient-autonomous-protection]] entries auto-apply by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH auto; MEDIUM pending; LOW informational) — `managed` posture REFUSES auto-apply for ALL severities.

url.description: Feed source URL. HTTPS recommended; file:// accepted for air-gap + tests.

publisher_pubkey.description: For verification_mode=ed25519: the pinned publisher pubkey as PEM OR `ed25519:<base64>` short form (`iam-jit-feed-publish init` emits the short form). Required for ed25519 mode; ignored for cosign-keyless mode.

verification_mode.description: ed25519 (canonical, no key management beyond pinning the pubkey) or cosign-keyless (per #441 Sysdig research; requires `cosign` binary on PATH + cosign_identity + cosign_issuer).

severity_auto_apply_threshold.description: Minimum severity for auto-apply on this feed. CRITICAL+HIGH auto-apply (with HIGH adding stderr notify); MEDIUM always queues; LOW always informational. Knob lets operator pin different thresholds per feed (community vs internal).

cosign_identity.description: Required when verification_mode=cosign-keyless. Pins the OIDC subject (e.g. 'releases@iam-jit.com' OR 'trsreagan3@gmail.com').

cosign_issuer.description: Required when verification_mode=cosign-keyless. Pins the OIDC issuer (e.g. 'https://accounts.google.com').

nickname.description: Optional human-readable label for log lines + `iam-jit updates last-fetch` output.

deployment_target_block.description: One named deployment-target (e.g. `prod-k8s`). `bouncer` identifies which Bounce-suite product (`ibounce`/`kbouncer`/`dbounce`/`gbounce`) owns this target; `classifier` declares the scope dimensions an agent can use to filter a long-range audit-query by deployment-target. Per [[multi-account-region-cluster-use-case]] classifiers cover ALL the dimensions a multi-account+multi-region+multi-cluster operator routinely scopes against.

bouncer.description: Which Bounce-suite product hosts this deployment-target. Determines which bouncer's `/audit/events` an agent queries for events from this target.

description.description: Optional human-readable description (surfaced in `iam-jit deployment-targets list` output).

classifier.description: Scope dimensions identifying which events belong to this deployment-target. The agent reads this block to build a `--scope-filter` argument for `iam-jit audit query --since 2y`. Multiple dimensions intersect (e.g. clusters AND accounts). Glob `*` is supported in every list entry.

clusters.description: K8s cluster names (kbouncer scope). Globs allowed (e.g. `prod-*`). Used to filter kbouncer events by `unmapped.iam_jit.cluster`.

accounts.description: AWS account IDs (ibounce scope; kbouncer IRSA scope). Used to filter events by `cloud.account.uid`.

regions.description: AWS / cloud regions (ibounce scope). Used to filter events by `cloud.region`.

hosts.description: Hostnames (gbounce scope; dbounce scope). Globs allowed (e.g. `*.prod.example.com`). Used to filter events by `dst_endpoint.hostname`.

retention_block.description: #428 / §A67 — compliance retention tiering. Pick a framework via `compliance:` to seed per-framework defaults, then optionally override individual fields. PCI/HIPAA/SOX/GDPR cover the common regulated workloads; `custom` skips defaults so the operator specifies every field.

compliance.description: Per-framework defaults (cumulative age thresholds in days). PCI: hot≤30 / warm≤120 / cold≤365 / no purge. HIPAA: hot≤30 / warm≤210 / cold≤2190 / purge 2190 (6 years). SOX: hot≤30 / warm≤395 / cold≤2555 / no purge (7-year floor). GDPR: hot≤30 / warm≤120 / cold≤365 / write-time PII purge. Custom: same shape as PCI, operator overrides every field.

hot_days.description: Days a rotated archive stays in the hot tier (locally queryable). Must be > 0.

warm_days.description: Days an archive stays warm (compressed local) after leaving hot. 0 disables the warm tier.

cold_days.description: Days an archive stays cold (eligible for S3 archival) after leaving warm. 0 disables the cold tier.

purge_after_days.description: Days at which an archive is unconditionally purged. null = keep indefinitely past cold. When set, MUST be >= hot+warm+cold so no data within the declared retention window is destroyed.

gdpr_pii_purge.description: When true, write-time PII redaction runs before disk + hot→warm archive transitions re-scrub the archive. True by default for the GDPR framework.

resource_mapping_block.description: One named resource mapping. `account_id` + `region` are exact-match substitution maps. `name_patterns` are ordered glob-style rewrites applied to resource names (first match wins; `*` is the only glob meta-char, with positional alignment between match + replace templates).

account_id.description: AWS account ID substitution map (exact match). YAML mapping `{ "source_acct": "target_acct" }`.

region.description: AWS region substitution map (exact match). Applied to ARN region positional fields + observed_scope.regions.

replace.description: Template with `*` placeholders aligned positionally to the captures from `match` (e.g. `prod-*`).

name_patterns.description: Ordered glob rewrites applied to the resource-name tail of an ARN (or the bare string for pseudo-ARNs). First matching pattern wins.

audit_chain_block.description: #500 / §A66c — Phase F audit-chain opt-in. When enabled=true the AuditLogWriter stamps every event with `unmapped.iam_jit.audit_chain.{seq,prev_hash,hash}` so `iam-jit audit verify` detects tampering. State persists across restarts at <log_dir>/audit-chain-state.json. Symmetric with the --audit-chain CLI flag.

audit_sign_manifests_block.description: #500 / §A66c — Phase F manifest-signing opt-in. When enabled=true the bouncer emits Ed25519-signed manifests every `interval_events` chain rows. Detects TAIL truncation that the chain alone cannot catch. Requires `audit_chain.enabled: true`. Symmetric with the --audit-sign-manifests CLI flag.

interval_events.description: Manifest cadence in events. Omitted = use the shipped default (1000).

keypair_dir.description: Directory holding the Ed25519 keypair. Omitted = ~/.iam-jit/audit-keys/ (auto-generated on first use with .priv at 0o600).

anomaly_detection_block.description: Phase H — per-agent behavioral baseline + anomaly detector. Opt-in operator wedge. Per [[anomaly-detection-mode-phase-h]] the detector composes with #404 deny-classifier (cold-start fallback per F.2) + #407 threat-feed (severity weighting). Baseline storage uses DUAL-MODE rolling window + exponential decay per F.3.

enabled.description: Master switch. Default OFF — operators opt in deliberately per [[anomaly-detection-mode-phase-h]] (don't make this the default; ambient mode without anomaly_detection stays as-is).

mode.description: alert = let request through + emit OCSF anomaly_detected (default, friction-minimised). block = return structured deny + emit. Per [[scorer-is-ground-truth]] block-mode is ADVISORY; deterministic deny floor still wins on conflict.

sensitivity.description: Z-score sensitivity. low=3.0σ (rare flags) / medium=2.0σ (default) / high=1.5σ (more flags). Operators tune per false-positive tolerance.

baseline_window.description: Rolling-window length. Accepts `Nd` / `Nh` / `Nm` / `Ns` strings or positive integer seconds. 14d default; matches the per-agent observable window from #266 + the Polygraph 14d convention.

baseline_decay_rate.description: Exponential decay rate per period (24h periods). 0.96 default ≈ 14d half-life. Lower values forget faster (more sensitive to recent behavior shifts). Per F.3 dual-mode storage uses BOTH the rolling window AND this decay; the detector picks per-dimension.

min_actions_for_baseline.description: Baseline must have at least this many observations before z-score scoring is authoritative. Below this the detector returns `insufficient_data` UNLESS cold_start_fallback is true (then it consults the #404 classifier).

cold_start_fallback.description: F.2 — when the baseline is too small, fall back to the #404 deny-classifier + Phase C threat-feed catalog so the operator gets adversarial-pattern detection from day-1. Set false to honor `insufficient_data` strictly.
